what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that links a provider-registry channel entry to a board item via a board:// URI.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format.
No code symbols or explanatory text are present.
standouts: Attachment type is 'board_items' for the 'provider-registry' channel.
The payload is empty beyond the typed link.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with an att-* id.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone.
Uses a board:// URI scheme to reference an external board item.
concerns: The file contains no body or payload, so the nature of the attachment is unspecified.
The board:// target cannot be resolved from the file alone.
notes: A bare, timestamped metadata pointer that ties a provider-registry channel attachment to a specific board item.
questions: What content or artifact is this attachment meant to carry?
What is the semantic relationship between the provider-registry channel and board://item-abaaa924?
Who or what process added this attachment?
purpose_vs_actual: The frontmatter frames it as a board_items attachment, but the actual content is only a typed target pointer with no attached payload.
intention_signals: It appears intended to record that a provider-registry channel item has a board-related attachment pointing to board://item-abaaa924.
novelty: An attachment record that carries no body and only a typed URI pointer to a board item.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link between a provider-registry channel attachment and a board item, which a fused system can keep as a relationship edge.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record for provider-registry that references board item board://item-abaaa924 with no body content.
intention_for_embedding: To register a board_items attachment linking a provider-registry channel entry to a specific board item.
